title: MANGA NGE manga re released in 3 1 Omnibus by Viz
display_title: MANGA NGE manga re released in 3 1 Omnibus by Viz
wikitext:
[]: [MANGA] NGE manga re-released in 3-1 Omnibus by Viz             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Aug 04, 2012 8:47 pm Article source: http://www.animenewsnetwork.com/convention/2012/otakon/5 3-1 Omnibus Source: http://www.animenewsnetwork.com/bbs/phpBB2/viewtopic.php?p=2253297#2253297 I wonder if we have Carl to thank for this.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat Aug 04, 2012 10:32 pm Weird. I was there. I didn't see that. And when I asked about its availability, they didn't know anything about it.

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Sat Aug 04, 2012 11:18 pm Bloody hell, where is a copy of Vol. 9? It's the only one I'm missing!

[]:             Quote Post by Blue Monday » Sun Aug 05, 2012 3:12 am Pretty interesting, I guess... Just hanging on 13 to drop later this year! Bloody hell, where is a copy of Vol. 9? It's the only one I'm missing!

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Mon Aug 06, 2012 12:50 pm It would be easier if I lived in Australia, but I don't. Ironically Viz is based in San Francisco and I know where their HQ is, although they are largely nondescript. The next time someone goes to the Philippines I’ll have to remember to ask them to look for vol. 9.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Aug 06, 2012 12:58 pm Another source for the 3-1 release, Googled it after seeing it mentioned on ANN forums: http://www.amazon.co.uk/Neon-Genesis-Evangelion-3---1/dp/1421550792/ref=sr_1_7?s=books&ie=UTF8&qid=1344275754&sr=1-7 December 6th is the UK release date.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Aug 06, 2012 2:23 pm Let's see... 1) Japanese tankoubon... 2) American comics (standard and collectors) 3) 1st edition release (standard and collectors) 4) 2nd edition release 5) omnibus release? Alright... my shelf does not have any more room on it for this nonsense.

[]:             Quote Post by Blue Monday » Mon Aug 06, 2012 6:57 pm Curious if there are any differences between the first and second printings from Viz, like different/variant covers? It would be easier if I lived in Australia, but I don't. Ironically Viz is based in San Francisco and I know where their HQ is, although they are largely nondescript. The next time someone goes to the Philippines I’ll have to remember to ask them to look for vol. 9.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Oct 03, 2012 11:33 am Omnibus information according to Amazon: Volume 1: http://www.amazon.com/dp/1421550792/ Volume 2: http://www.amazon.com/dp/1421553058/ Volume 3: http://www.amazon.com/dp/1421553627/ Apparently they'll contain the color pages from the start of each volume this time around. I hope they'll look over the translation because I know Volume 8 was wonky in places.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Tue Nov 13, 2012 6:54 pm So does anyone have the first omnibus? It was supposed to be released today. I can't find anything official, or anything akin to a photograph of the actual final product.

[]:             Quote Post by Blue Monday » Wed Nov 14, 2012 7:51 am I (pre-)ordered it from BDUK, but I don't expect it in the mail for another week or two at least. I just reread the manga like a month ago but I'll smash this lil' thing when it arrives, haha.

[]:             Quote Post by AR-99 » Wed Nov 14, 2012 12:24 pm Here's Viz's product page for it: http://www.viz.com/product?id=10415 Very dated response to Blue Monday: Tempting, but the shipping and such would kill it probably. Picking up other Eva stuff only available in Oz would justify the expense but neither are of much priority.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Nov 14, 2012 10:37 pm Hey kids, I got my hands on a copy: http://www.evamonkey.com/the-evangelion-omnibus-is-a-real-thing/

[]:             Quote Post by Blue Monday » Thu Nov 15, 2012 5:56 am Nice. Stoked about the the slightly larger format. I thought for sure we'd lose out on the colour pages, but nope... Sounds like a solid release. EM, this is sort of a weird question; but how's the binding? I've found with a lot of "omnibus" books it can be tricky to keep from cracking or putting creases in the spine whilst reading.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Nov 15, 2012 9:47 am Blue Monday wrote: how's the binding?

[]:             Quote Post by EvangelionFan » Thu Nov 15, 2012 7:00 pm I don't go to comic stores often anymore, but the Madman copies are usually in better stock than the Viz editions, which are also sold here. According to their site, all 12 volumes are still in stock - but no note about the omnibus editions or Volume 13 on their release schedule, hard to say why, except that I've observed they released their edition of vol. 12 a little later than the Viz edition.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Nov 15, 2012 7:23 pm EvangelionFan wrote: the Madman copies are usually in better stock than the Viz editions, which are also sold here

[]:             Quote Post by EvangelionFan » Thu Nov 15, 2012 7:34 pm The Madman editions are the ones with the cover jackets.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Nov 15, 2012 8:18 pm I read the 3-1 Vol.1 version today. It was an interesting feel to read it back-to-back with Vol. 13 (--also made me notice the various Manga nods that NME 1.0 includes). I'm not a big enough fan of the Manga to purchase it (something about it is harder for me to get into, in part I think the obvious lack of animation for battle scenes and my ambivalence about Shinji's manga portrayal) but I think NGE Manga fans should pick it up. The various commentaries from the original releases and included color artwork make it a great deal. Super minor nitpick is how the 3-1 version has Rei say "Ikari" while Vol 13 has "Ikari-kun". Consistency, people!

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Thu Nov 15, 2012 8:38 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: Super minor nitpick is how the 3-1 version has Rei say "Ikari" while Vol 13 has "Ikari-kun". Consistency, people!
